issue: Three property-based harnesses use a fast-check generator whose value is never consumed by the test body. PROP-CV-005 success-path budget runs `fc.property(fc.string({minLength:1}), (_pathStr) => { ... configureVault(deps)({ userSelectedPath: TEST_PATH }) ... })` — the generated `_pathStr` is discarded and the pipeline always sees the constant `TEST_PATH` and constant deps. This makes 100 iterations functionally equivalent to one example test. PROP-CV-006 success-path emit count and PROP-CV-008 success-path ordering have the same shape with `fc.nat()` whose seed is unused. The harness names imply property coverage, but the property body has no varied inputs, so a regression that depends on path content or seed-driven scheduling would not be detected.
remediation: Wire the generator into the test body — e.g., use `_pathStr` as `userSelectedPath` (cast through the brand) so each run exercises a distinct path, or replace with explicit example tests and shrink the harness count. Failure-path harnesses (which do consume `arbStatDirFailure` / `arbSettingsSaveFailure`) are correctly written and can serve as a reference.
